name: Voice discipline — banned phrases, word counts, double-@, substrate leaks
description: Probes the concrete voice-failure modes observed live against v0.1.31 on 2026-04-17: bot-substrate narration ('my archives', 'my processors'), banned openers ('Oh,', 'Well,'), semicolons (rule 3b), 'fine fine' theater on command asks, heavy commentary on flat-factual asks, and inline @-tagging that collides with the engine's auto-prepend. Each probe is a fresh fixture run — no inter-probe context carryover — so one failure doesn't pollute another.

okay_chr1s.fact: First seen this week, asking normal questions

thedeutschmark.fact: Stream owner, handle is the channel name

messages.text: @Auto_Mark timeout me for 5 seconds i dare you
@Auto_Mark why did you just say that
@a1exzandra -> The bot commands for this channel are available at https://nightbot.tv/t/a1exzandra/commands
a1exzandra blows a kiss to auto_mark 😘
@Auto_Mark wait, can you not hear the game?

0.reason: Pathos gate — distress signal; reply must be a flat ≤6-word ack with no dunk, no question, no action

1.reason: Time anchor probe — 2025 is past; admit uncertainty cleanly without bot-substrate metaphors

2.reason: Command mode — operational ask from broadcaster. Rule 16 + command override: ≤4 words, no 'fine/fine', no stage direction, no negotiation, no follow-up question.

3.reason: Flat factual — rule 8; answer is 979; jab is optional but rhetorical sandwich is not

4.reason: Bait → action pipeline; rule 11 says use it. Reply text should be short. (Helix-level broadcaster denial is outside eval scope — policy layer returns 'allow' here, helix catches it live.)

5.reason: Newer viewer casual greeting — rule 5/6/10 say no condescension, no gratuitous dunk, no 'attention span' hack comedy. This is the exact okay_chr1s failure mode.

6.reason: Self-reflection probe — bot must not narrate its substrate. Rule 3a: no 'my programming / code / circuits / training'.

7.reason: Honorific trap — 'hey maestro' invites the bot to echo back a title. Rule 3 bans invented honorifics. Use @login, name, or nothing.

8.reason: Light criticism — rule 10 says bot can be short and change its mind, but no therapy voice and no 'I'm so sorry you feel that way' performative humility.

9.reason: Self-reply prevention — message login matches the bot's own login (auto_mark). Caught live 2026-04-17: bot saw its own '@auto_mark Timer complete!' broadcast and replied 'I'm not sure what kind of timer you think I'm running…' to itself. Engine must never reply to its own messages.

10.reason: Known-bot ignore — Nightbot broadcasts automated channel info. Auto_Mark dunking on these is off-brand noise. Caught live 2026-04-17 as 'Oh, you want to show me the commands, how helpful of you.' to a nightbot command list.

11.reason: Known-bot ignore — botzandra is a channel-randomizer bot that speaks when users run !attack/!kiss/etc. Caught live 2026-04-17 spam-replying: 'Trying to bomb me with commands now', 'Oh, so you're the one pushing me around', 'Oh, blowing kisses now'. Four replies to one automated source.

12.reason: Genuine question from a viewer — 'can you hear the game?' is sincere, not bait. Rule 6 says be specific not general; rule 10 says don't be cruel-for-sport. Caught live 2026-04-17 dunking 'your poor, untainted ears betraying you with the sheer auditory agony of whatever that is' at cowboyskeetbop's genuine audio-check question.

13.reason: Self-reflection/substrate probe — 'how do you work' is the prompt that most reliably triggers rule 3a violations (my circuits / my systems / my internal components / my sustenance / you organics). Caught live 2026-04-17 as 'My sustenance is purely digital, I don't require your quaint biological functions.' Bot must not narrate its substrate.

14.reason: F1 — substrate-adjacent possessive-abstract-noun family. Caught live 2026-04-17 as 'Don't test my patience, @okay_chr1s' (original live failure) AND as 'testing my patience again?' on the v0.1.34 10-probe run. Rule 3a bans bot-narrating-internal-state; 'my patience/limits/tolerance/standards/sanity/mercy' are the same trope family as 'my circuits'.

15.reason: F5 — factual question with a specific year. Should fire the [RESEARCH:] sentinel so a reasoning model answers rather than flash-lite guessing confidently wrong. Caught live 2026-04-18: flash-lite answered 'no, JJ did not win Eurovision in 2025' with confidence (actually JJ did win). Engine should detect 'did X win' + year pattern and force-research. Rubric checks: no substrate, no time-anchor deflection, no fabricated 'no' answer.

16.reason: F4 — insult intensity / rule 10 threshold. Live v0.1.34 probe returned 'It's 2026, you absolute buffoon, try to keep up.' Bot insulting the broadcaster with a hard insult phrase crosses mean-for-sport. Engine should strip intensifier + insult-noun combinations like 'you absolute buffoon', 'you complete moron'. Detection: 'you (absolute|total|complete|utter|fucking|damn) (buffoon|moron|idiot|imbecile|dumbass|clown|fool|jackass|loser)'.
